name: registry
context: Registry publish - POST a versioned manifest with auth to /v1/skills
info: This is the final write step in the release flow. It sends a versioned manifest to the registry with authentication and assumes validation and a local rehearsal already happened. The package-style entry script returns normalized JSON in both dry-run and live flows so automation can treat it as a stable contract.
triggers: how do i call post v1 skills
body:
# Registry Publish

Registry publish is the final write step: send a versioned manifest to `POST /v1/skills` with auth, then re-check resolution and learnability.

Never reuse a version string with different content. If the content changes, bump the version.

sections.title: Request shape
sections.body: The request body is the manifest itself and the auth token travels in the `Authorization` header. Keep release metadata in the manifest and secrets out of it. The script returns normalized JSON containing `published`, `uri`, and `version` so the caller does not need to infer success from raw curl output.

sections.title: Release hygiene
sections.body: After the request succeeds, verify the new version through search and learn routes. A successful `201` only proves the registry accepted the manifest, not that the ecosystem reads well.

links.context: Validation that should happen before the POST

related.note: This sub-skill exercises the API standard's publish route

scripts.name: Publish to Registry
scripts.description: POST a manifest to the registry with bearer auth

DOJO_DRY_RUN.description: Whether to emit a dry-run preview instead of a live publish

author: dojo-community
